note: Recognizes party autonomy to select the law governing their contract, subject to limited exceptions (e.g. no substantial relationship and no reasonable basis for the choice, or violation of a fundamental policy of a state with a materially greater interest).

citation: M/S Bremen v. Zapata Off-Shore Co., 407 U.S. 1 (1972)
note: Established that forum-selection clauses are prima facie valid and enforceable in U.S. federal courts absent a strong showing that enforcement would be unreasonable.
